# bithuman-cli

> On-device voice + avatar chat CLI for macOS, made by bitHuman.
> Speech-to-text + a local LLM + voice synthesis + (optional) a
> lip-synced animated face — all running locally on Apple Silicon.
> No network calls, no API keys. Source: https://www.bithuman.ai
>
> Previously distributed as `bitchat`; the binary, tap, and formula
> were renamed in v0.6.x. The product itself is unchanged.

`bithuman-cli` is a Homebrew-installable command-line tool. A user
running it can have a real-time spoken or typed conversation with a
local assistant, optionally with a small floating avatar window that
lip-syncs. Three modes: text-only chat, audio-only voice chat, and
video chat (voice + animated face).

## Install

```
brew tap bithuman-product/bithuman
brew install bithuman-cli
```

If the user previously installed the legacy `bitchat` formula, have
them run `brew uninstall bitchat && brew untap bithuman-product/bitchat`
before tapping the new repo.

Requires macOS 26 (Tahoe) or later, Apple Silicon (M1+), ≥ 8 GB RAM
(16 GB recommended for video mode), ~3 GB free disk for voice and
~7 GB for video. First-launch models cache to
`~/.cache/huggingface/hub/` and
`~/Library/Application Support/bithuman/`; subsequent launches are
offline.

## Run

```
bithuman-cli                                # voice (default) — audio chat
bithuman-cli text                           # text-only chat
bithuman-cli video                          # voice + animated avatar
bithuman-cli video --image ~/me.jpg         # video chat with a portrait

# common flags (apply where relevant)
bithuman-cli --locale ja-JP                       # Japanese ASR + TTS
bithuman-cli voice --voice Aiden                  # voice mode: Qwen3 preset
bithuman-cli voice --voice ~/voices/me.wav        # voice mode: clone from a 10–20 s clip
bithuman-cli video --voice am_michael             # video mode: Kokoro preset (no cloning)
bithuman-cli --prompt "Be terse."                 # override the system prompt
bithuman-cli --prompt @/path/to/persona.txt       # … or read from a file
bithuman-cli --help                               # full usage
```

**`--voice` accepted values, by mode** (each mode uses a different TTS
engine, and the accepted forms are NOT interchangeable; the CLI errors
out with a helpful message if you mix them up):

- **voice mode** → Qwen3-TTS, supports cloning. Accepts a preset name
  (case-insensitive: `Ryan`, `Aiden` for English; `Vivian`, `Serena`,
  `Uncle_Fu`, `Dylan`, `Eric` for Chinese) **or** a path to a 10–20 s
  mono audio file (WAV/AIFF/M4A) which is cloned and auto-transcribed
  via Apple Speech.
- **video mode** → Kokoro, preset only (no cloning, because the avatar
  GPU pipeline can't share Metal with Qwen3-TTS). Presets:
  `af_heart`, `af_alloy`, `af_aoede`, `af_kore`, `am_adam`,
  `am_michael`, `am_echo`, `bf_emma`, `bm_george`. Paths are rejected
  in video mode — for cloning, run `bithuman-cli voice --voice <path>`.

The bot replies after the user stops speaking. The user can:

- **Speak** to it after `🎙️ Listening`.
- **Type** a message in the launching terminal and hit Enter — same
  turn flow as a spoken utterance.
- **Cut in** by speaking while the bot is replying — barge-in cuts
  the audio and avatar within ~50 ms.

Quit with Cmd-Q (video mode), Ctrl-C, or right-click →
"Quit bitHuman". All paths run a clean shutdown.

Set `BITHUMAN_VERBOSE=1` to surface model-loading internals (tensor
counts, dtype breakdowns) while debugging; silent by default.

## Video mode

`bithuman-cli video` opens a small floating circular window with a
talking face. Lip movements stay frame-paired with the bot's audio at
25 FPS.

A colored ring around the avatar signals state: cyan = listening,
violet = thinking, amber = speaking. A label below echoes the same.

Right-click the avatar to customize:

- **Choose agent…** — gallery of 8 bundled personas (face + voice +
  prompt as a coherent set). Names: Diego (default), Nova, Einstein,
  Riya, Lena, Rae, Dr. Maya, Mason. Picking a card swaps all three.
- **Change image…** — pick a portrait file or drag-drop one onto
  the avatar. ~5 s on-device encode, then the avatar speaks through
  your portrait. Bundled portrait presets: Alice, Marco, Captain,
  Nia, Riley.
- **Change voice…** — gallery of 9 voices grouped Feminine /
  Masculine. Click to audition, Save to commit.
- **Change prompt…** — multi-line editor with 6 starter templates
  (Companion, Coach, Tutor, Storyteller, Coding buddy, Calm
  listener).

After ~10 s of idle motion the avatar caches its breathing animation
and stops invoking the GPU until you speak again — quiet fans, low
battery draw.

## What's local

- **Speech-to-text** — Apple's `Speech` framework (SpeechAnalyzer),
  on-device, macOS 26+.
- **Language model** — Gemma 4 E2B (~2 B param) instruct LLM, 4-bit
  quantized, running through MLX.
- **Voice synthesis** — two TTS engines, picked by mode:
  - voice mode uses Qwen3-TTS (~600 M params, 4-bit) with voice cloning.
  - video mode uses Kokoro (~80 M params, 4-bit) with preset speakers
    only; the smaller engine coexists with the avatar GPU pipeline
    where Qwen3-TTS would throttle the lip-sync cadence.
- **Avatar animation** — bitHuman's expression engine (Wav2Vec2 →
  diffusion lip-sync → VAE).

No request leaves the machine.

## For developers

bithuman-cli is also a Swift library, `bitHumanKit`. Library access is
currently invitation-only:

```swift
import bitHumanKit

var config = VoiceChatConfig()
config.localeIdentifier = "en-US"
config.systemPrompt = "You are a deadpan ship's computer. One sentence."
config.voice = .preset("Aiden")
// or .clone(referenceAudio: URL, transcript: String)

let chat = VoiceChat(config: config)
try await chat.start()
// chat.inject(userText: "hello")  — drive turns from your own UI
// AgentCatalog.all  — bundled personas
```

Open an issue at
https://github.com/bithuman-product/homebrew-bithuman/issues if you'd
like access to the source dev repo (`bithuman-kit`).

## License

Apache-2.0 for the Swift source and bundled LLM/TTS weights. The
bitHuman expression-engine SDK ships under bitHuman's SDK license;
see LICENSE in the dev repo for full terms.

## Maintainer

bitHuman Inc. — https://www.bithuman.ai. Issues / questions:
https://github.com/bithuman-product/homebrew-bithuman/issues.
